topic: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional

sections.title: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional — Introduction and Formula
sections.hindiTitle: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional — परिचय और फॉर्मूला
sections.explanation: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional is an essential building block of English grammar. Formula: Zero: If + present simple, present simple (general truth) | First: If + present simple, will + base verb (real future possibility). Rules: Zero conditional describes general facts/scientific truths that are always true | First conditional describes a real, likely future situation and its result | Both clauses can switch order without changing meaning (comma only needed when 'if' clause comes first) | Do not use 'will' in the if-clause
sections.formula: Zero: If + present simple, present simple (general truth) | First: If + present simple, will + base verb (real future possibility)

examples.hindi: अगर पानी 100 डिग्री तक गर्म होता है, तो वह उबलता है।
examples.english: If water heats to 100 degrees, it boils.

examples.hindi: अगर तुम बर्फ को गर्म करते हो, तो वह पिघल जाती है।
examples.english: If you heat ice, it melts.

examples.hindi: अगर मुझे समय मिला, तो मैं आऊँगा।
examples.english: If I get time, I will come.

examples.hindi: अगर बारिश हुई, तो हम घर पर रहेंगे।
examples.english: If it rains, we will stay home.

examples.hindi: अगर तुम मेहनत करोगे, तो सफल होगे।
examples.english: If you work hard, you will succeed.

examples.hindi: अगर वह जल्दी आया, तो हम शुरू करेंगे।
examples.english: If he comes early, we will start.

sections.title: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional — Daily Life Usage
sections.hindiTitle: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional — रोज़मर्रा के उपयोग
sections.explanation: Everyday sentences using If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional for daily conversation.
sections.formula: Zero: If + present simple, present simple (general truth) | First: If + present simple, will + base verb (real future possibility)

examples.hindi: अगर सूरज निकलता है, तो दिन गर्म होता है।
examples.english: If the sun comes out, the day is warm.

examples.hindi: अगर तुम यह खाओगे, तो तुम्हें अच्छा लगेगा।
examples.english: If you eat this, you will feel good.

examples.hindi: अगर तुम्हें मदद चाहिए, तो मुझे बताओ।
examples.english: If you need help, tell me.

examples.hindi: अगर वह नहीं आया, तो हम शुरू करेंगे।
examples.english: If he doesn't come, we will start.

examples.hindi: अगर तुम गर्मी में बाहर जाते हो, तो पसीना आता है।
examples.english: If you go out in summer, you sweat.

examples.hindi: अगर मैं यह exam पास करूँ, तो मैं खुश होऊँगा।
examples.english: If I pass this exam, I will be happy.

sections.title: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional — Office & Professional Usage
sections.hindiTitle: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional — ऑफिस और पेशेवर उपयोग
sections.explanation: Practice If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional in professional and office contexts.
sections.formula: Zero: If + present simple, present simple (general truth) | First: If + present simple, will + base verb (real future possibility)

examples.hindi: अगर पानी नहीं मिलेगा, तो पौधे मर जाएंगे।
examples.english: If plants don't get water, they die.

examples.hindi: अगर तुम जल्दी सोओगे, तो जल्दी उठोगे।
examples.english: If you sleep early, you will wake up early.

examples.hindi: अगर तुम्हें यह नहीं समझ आया, तो पूछो।
examples.english: If you don't understand this, ask.

examples.hindi: अगर धूप निकलती है, तो बर्फ पिघलती है।
examples.english: If the sun comes out, snow melts.

sections.title: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional — Negative and Question Forms
sections.hindiTitle: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional — नकारात्मक और प्रश्न रूप
sections.explanation: How to make negative sentences and questions using If Clauses / Conditionals Part 1 — Zero & First Conditional. Common mistakes are highlighted here.
sections.formula: Zero: If + present simple, present simple (general truth) | First: If + present simple, will + base verb (real future possibility)

examples.hindi: अगर तुम यह करोगे, तो मैं खुश होऊँगा।
examples.english: If you do this, I will be happy.

examples.hindi: अगर बिजली चली जाती है, तो कमरा अंधेरा हो जाता है।
examples.english: If the electricity goes off, the room becomes dark.

examples.hindi: अगर तुम practice करोगे, तो बेहतर बनोगे।
examples.english: If you practice, you will get better.

examples.hindi: अगर मुझे मौका मिला, तो मैं विदेश जाऊँगा।
examples.english: If I get a chance, I will go abroad.
